title: Notify users that a BoltDB database still exists
body:
With Podman 6, we removed all BoltDB support. To try and ease the transition, Podman 5.8 is capable migrating a Bolt database to the new SQLite database that we've defaulted to since late in the Podman 4.x release series. However - what happens if someone goes from Podman 5.7 or lower, straight to Podman 6.x? No migration will occur, and the user loses all their containers.

Step 1 of a fix is just to tell folks that they have a legacy database in place, and they really need to go back to 5.8 and do a migration. Step 2 is to introduce some sort of migration binary (probably a very stripped-down Podman 5.8?) that we can ship alongside Podman 6 (maybe as a subpackage?) so that folks can migrate without a downgrade.

<!--

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

We already cover this in the Podman 6 BoltDB removal PR, so...

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: tmt tests failed for commit 064084f12abfa79509ed88c3d8a54cee89c48c95. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

For stage 2 of this: Instead of using a migration binary, how would y'all feel about integrating a subset of BoltDB back in as a subpackage of Libpod, so it can be called exclusively to do the migration? I tried slimming down Podman to make a migration-only version of 5.8, but it got bad - you need to be able to stop containers (to account for a 5 to 6 upgrade on a running system, where we need to do an immediate `system migrate`), and stop requires... basically the entirety of Libpod? I can't get more than 10% of the binary size off without starting to degrade functionality we actually need.

If there are no objections to my proposed "re-add super-minimal boltdb just for migration" idea: I'm going to close this and just go with that instead. It's a lot more reasonable than a dedicated migration binary. We should be able to finally yank the migration code with 7 in two years?
With no comments against, I'm closing this in favor of #28569
